description: One who guards, maintains, or preserves something; a protector of what matters. 'Woru' names who holds safe—maintaining what must not be lost.
sound_symbolism: The word watches with care. 'Wo' opens with watchful attention, vigilance awake. 'Ru' holds with steady guardianship, protection maintained.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo woru' (keepers). Related to 'theluo' (steward) but emphasizes guarding over managing. Protects 'moenu' (culture) and 'therema' (tradition).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Keeper as protector—guarding what supports flourishing without grasping.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Keeper of knowledge—those who guard traditions and pass them on.
